back: Web crawling is the automated process of systematically browsing the World Wide Web to download and index web pages. A program that performs this task is called a web crawler or spider.

front: What is the main purpose of the Robots Exclusion Protocol (robots.txt)?
back: The Robots Exclusion Protocol is a standard used by websites to communicate with web crawlers. It specifies which parts of the site should not be processed or scanned by compliant crawlers, helping to manage server load and protect sensitive areas.

front: What is the difference between a web directory and a search engine?
back: A web directory is a human-curated catalog of websites organized into categories. A search engine, in contrast, uses automated algorithms (crawlers and indexers) to build a massive, searchable index of web page content.

front: What is the 'Crawl Frontier' in web crawling?
back: The crawl frontier is the set of URLs that have been discovered but not yet visited or downloaded by the crawler. It is typically managed as a priority queue to determine the order in which pages are fetched.

front: What is a major challenge in web data management known as 'Page Rank'?
back: PageRank is a link analysis algorithm used by Google Search to rank web pages. It assigns a numerical weight to each element of a hyperlinked set of documents, with the purpose of 'measuring' its relative importance within the set. It is a key concept in combating the challenge of determining page quality and authority.
